channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Ex-NFL star implores Eagles to celebrate title at White House, says it would be 'ludicrous' not to
item.description: Former NFL star cornerback Adam "Pacman" Jones said the Philadelphia Eagles, or any team, should go to the White House to celebrate the Super Bowl title.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Aaron Rodgers’ potential landing spots as Jets prepare to move on
item.description: The Aaron Rodgers era in New York has come to a quick and abrupt end. But the veteran quarterback could be a good option for several NFL teams if he decides to return for a 21st season.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Georgia fans show it's possible to be polite and still celebrate win over No. 3 Florida on the court
item.description: ATHENS, Ga. (AP) — Georgia fans showed it's possible to storm the court for a postgame celebration — and to be polite at the same time. The Stegeman Coliseum public address announcer made repeated requests in the final minute of Georgia's 88-83 upset of No.
item.dc:creator: CHARLES ODUM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Lionel Messi strikes early, Inter Miami beats Sporting KC 3-1 to advance in Champions Cup
item.description: FORT LAUDERDALE, Fla. (AP) — Lionel Messi opened the scoring with a masterful strike in the 19th minute, and Inter Miami beat Sporting Kansas City 3-1 on Tuesday night in the second leg of the CONCACAF Champions Cup’s first round.
item.dc:creator: ALANIS THAMES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Maine legislature censures Rep. Laurel Libby for pointing out trans athlete who won girls' competition
item.description: The Maine House of Representatives voted to censure Rep. Laurel Libby for pointing out that a trans athlete won a girls' pole vault competition.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Georgia recovers from No. 3 Florida's comeback and defeat Gators 88-83 despite Richard's 30 points
item.description: ATHENS, Ga. (AP) — Georgia guard Blue Cain sank a go-ahead 3-pointer with 47 seconds remaining after No. 3 Florida took its first lead by scoring 13 unanswered points, and the Bulldogs beat the Gators 88-83 on Tuesday night.
item.dc:creator: CHARLES ODUM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: CFP commissioners put off any decisions on changes to playoff format, including seeding of teams
item.description: DALLAS (AP) — Conference commissioners who are part of the College Football Playoff had discussions Tuesday about possible changes for next season, including how the 12-team field is seeded.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN HAWKINS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Giants general manager discusses 'taking swings' for quarterback as Matthew Stafford rumors heat up
item.description: It's no secret the New York Giants need a quarterback, and general manager Joe Schoen said he is not opposed to "taking swings" to find his guy.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Howie Roseman says the Eagles' roster will change as team begins quest for repeat title
item.description: INDIANAPOLIS (AP) — Howie Roseman’s forehead has healed from the beer can that left him bloodied at the Philadelphia Eagles’ victory parade, and the general manager is working on getting the team in position to repeat.
item.dc:creator: ROB MAADDI, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Chelsea routs Southampton 4-0 on frustrating night for Cole Palmer. Brighton and Palace win
item.description: Chelsea wasn't so reliant on Cole Palmer this time. On a frustrating evening for the England star, his Chelsea teammates stepped up — as demanded by their manager — to share the goals in a 4-0 thrashing of last-place Southampton in the Premier League on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: STEVE DOUGLAS, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Diana Taurasi of the Phoenix Mercury retires after 20 WNBA seasons, 3 titles and 6 Olympic golds
item.description: Diana Taurasi is retiring after 20 seasons, ending one of the greatest careers in women’s basketball history.
item.dc:creator: John Marshall And Doug Feinberg, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Khalil Shakir, Bills agree to 4-year contract extension
item.description: The Buffalo Bills announced they signed wide receiver Khalil Shakir to a four-year contract extension on Tuesday, as they keep Josh Allen's top target after his career year.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Wizards finally hold a team under 100 points, the last NBA team to do so this season
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — It took 57 games for the Washington Wizards to hold a team under 100 points this season. Of course, Marcus Smart wasn't around for most of those. Smart and the Wizards held Brooklyn to 12 points in the fourth quarter of their 107-99 victory over the Nets on Monday night.
item.dc:creator: NOAH TRISTER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Texas is No. 1 in women's AP Top 25 for the first time in 21 years; NC State climbs into top 10
item.description: Texas is No. 1 in The Associated Press Top 25 women's basketball poll for the first time in 21 years and became the third different team to hold the top spot in the past three weeks. The Longhorns moved atop the poll Monday after previous No. 1 Notre Dame lost in double overtime to N.C.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FEINBERG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Morrissey, Scheifele rally Jets to 2-1 overtime win over Sharks for 10th straight
item.description: WINNIPEG, Manitoba (AP) — Josh Morrissey scored the tying goal in the final minute of regulation and Mark Scheifele got the winner 1:33 into overtime as the Winnipeg Jets rallied to beat the San Jose Sharks 2-1 on Monday night for their franchise-record 10th straight win.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Chicago Sky acquire 2-time All-Star Atkins from Washington Mystics for 2025 No. 3 pick
item.description: The Chicago Sky have traded their top pick in the upcoming WNBA draft for two-time All-Star guard Ariel Atkins from the Washington Mystics.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Keyonte George scores 30 in Jazz win over Rockets
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - Keyonte George scored 30 points and Lauri Markkanen added 23 points and 10 rebounds to rally the Utah Jazz to a 124-115 victory over the Houston Rockets on Saturday night.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: RSL gets shut out in MLS season opener by San Jose
item.description: SAN JOSE, Calif. (ABC4 Sports) - From San Jose, Costa Rica to San Jose, California, Real Salt Lake has not been able to score a goal in this early part of the 2025 season. After a scoreless draw in the CONCACAF Champions Cup opener against C.S.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Utah State sweeps San Diego State, 79-71
item.description: LOGAN, Utah (ABC4 Sports) - For just the second time first since joining the Mountain West Conference, Utah State sweeps San Diego State. Dexter Akanno scored a career-high 26 points, making 7-of-9 three-pointers to lead the Aggies to a 79-71 win over the Aztecs Saturday night at The Spectrum.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: One-armed basketball player makes history with basket
item.description: When her high school basketball coach cut her from the team senior year, Baileigh Sinaman-Daniel refused to let that rejection, or the fact she was born with one arm, end her dream.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Victor Wembanyama expected to miss remainder of season after blood clot diagnosis, Spurs announce
item.description: San Antonio Spurs star Victor Wembanyama is expected to miss the remainder of the NBA regular season after being diagnosed with a blood clot in his right shoulder, the Spurs announced Thursday. It's a massive blow to the Spurs, who are contending for a playoff spot in the Western Conference.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: RSL settles for scoreless draw in Champions Cup opener
item.description: SAN JOSE, Costa Rica (ABC4 Sports) - Real Salt Lake kicked off its 2025 CONCACAF Champions Cup with a scoreless draw against reigning Costa Rican champions CS Herediano in RSL's first Champions Cup game since 2016. "I thought it was a really hard fought game," said RSL head coach Pablo Mastroeni.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4
